# ExerciseResult { #script_widget.executor.ExerciseResult }

```python
script_widget.executor.ExerciseResult(
    stdout='',
    stderr='',
    outputs=None,
    error=None,
    traceback=None,
)
```

The outcome of running one ``%%exercise`` cell.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**stdout**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Everything the cell printed to stdout.

<code>[**stderr**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Everything the cell printed to stderr.

<code>[**outputs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [list of dict]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Rich display MIME bundles produced by the cell -- a trailing bare expression's repr, an explicit ``display(...)`` call, or (when run with a live IPython shell) a matplotlib figure flushed after execution. Each entry maps MIME type to its data, e.g. ``{"image/png": "<base64>", "text/plain": "..."}``.

<code>[**error**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`) or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A short ``"ExceptionType: message"`` summary if the cell failed to parse or raised while running, else ``None``.

<code>[**traceback**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`) or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The full formatted traceback for ``error``, else ``None``.
